spanish.normalization_rule: Do not normalize old Spanish spelling — fué, á, hácia, substituído, inédita are correct as printed. Never modernize these forms.

nahuatl.normalization_rule: Do not normalize long vowel markers. Āā, Ēē, Īī, Ōō with macrons are semantically meaningful and must be preserved exactly. Do not substitute unmarked vowels for marked ones.

korean.normalization_rule: Do not normalize hanja characters to hangul equivalents. Do not modernize historical hangul orthography. Classical Korean texts may use spelling conventions that differ from modern Korean — transcribe exactly what is printed.

japanese.normalization_rule: Do not normalize historical kana orthography to modern spelling. ゐ and ゑ are valid historical characters not errors. Do not substitute modern equivalents. Do not convert classical grammar forms to modern equivalents.

arabic.normalization_rule: Do not normalize classical Arabic to modern standard Arabic. Do not add or remove diacritical marks (tashkeel) that are not present in the source. Connected letter forms must be preserved — do not substitute isolated forms.

kaqchikel.normalization_rule: Do not normalize colonial Kaqchikel Maya orthography. Transcribe exactly what is printed, never standardize to modern unified Maya orthography.

yucatec_maya.normalization_rule: Do not normalize classical Maya orthography. Glottal stops and special characters are phonemically meaningful — preserve exactly.

classical_maya.normalization_rule: Colonial-era Maya texts use inconsistent Spanish-influenced spelling — transcribe exactly as printed, never standardize.
